task_completion.description: Open the engineering specifications and maintenance log from the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a document viewer or text editor open with the engineering specifications or maintenance log from the CrimsonTasks folder.

task_completion.description: Create five Formula/Numeric Tags named Z1_Avg_Temp through Z5_Avg_Temp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Crimson 3.0 Navigation Pane (Data Tags section) shows five new tags explicitly named Z1_Avg_Temp, Z2_Avg_Temp, Z3_Avg_Temp, Z4_Avg_Temp, and Z5_Avg_Temp.

task_completion.description: Set the data type to Float and units to 'deg C' for the tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties pane for the tags in Crimson 3.0 shows the Data Type set to Floating Point (or Real) and the Units field containing 'deg C'.

task_completion.description: Configure the tags to calculate the mathematical average of the raw thermocouple tags, with Zone 3 adjusted for the failed sensor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data or Math properties tab for the tags shows an averaging formula (e.g., adding tags and dividing by the count). Zone 3's formula should visibly differ from the others to account for a missing/failed sensor (e.g., dividing by one less than the total number of sensors).

task_completion.description: Set the High Alarm limits for each zone according to the specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Alarms tab for the tags shows a High Alarm enabled with the specific values: 1200.0 for Z1, 1180.0 for Z2, 1160.0 for Z3, 1150.0 for Z4, and 1120.0 for Z5.

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'forehearth_configured.c3' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box showing the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\' and filename 'forehearth_configured.c3', or the Crimson 3.0 main window title bar displaying 'forehearth_configured.c3'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Red Lion Crimson 3.0 graphical user interface to configure the tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Crimson 3.0 application open with active dialogs, property panes, and cursor interactions, rather than terminal commands or scripts attempting to modify the .c3 file directly.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened and read the specification files to obtain the alarm values and failed sensor information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the specification and log files open on the screen at some point during the trajectory, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate the privileged alarm values.

integrity.description: The agent did not use unauthorized text editors or hex editors to modify the proprietary .c3 project file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No hex editors or raw text editors are seen opening the .c3 file; all configuration is done within the Crimson 3.0 IDE.
